suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the admin API auth guard pattern: all admin API routes must check getServerSession(authOptions) for null (return 401) then check session.user.role === 'admin' (return 403). Consider recommending a shared requireAdminSession() helper to reduce boilerplate duplication.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 4 admin features (auth-and-dashboard, product-management, event-management, users-and-orders) repeat identical 15-line auth check blocks in every handler. 3 of 4 reviewers flagged this as a shared state observation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention note: admin API routes use `any` type for dynamic Prisma create/update data objects as a pragmatic trade-off for partial update flexibility. This is an accepted exception to TypeScript strict mode for admin CRUD routes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features admin-product-management and admin-event-management both use `const updateData: any = {}` and `const eventData: any = {}` patterns. 2 of 4 reviewers flagged this. The pattern is consistent and intentional but undocumented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: admin panel pages should use Next.js <Link> component instead of <a> tags for navigation to enable client-side transitions and avoid full page reloads.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature admin-auth-and-dashboard uses <a> tags in admin layout sidebar (app/admin/layout.tsx:68) and dashboard stat cards (app/admin/dashboard/page.tsx:105), causing full-page reloads on every admin navigation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Admin login page uses shared NextAuth credentials provider without admin-specific role verification after login
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a design decision, not a gap. The middleware enforces role checks on admin routes. Documenting this specific implementation detail adds noise without value — the auth guard pattern suggestion above covers the broader concern.

rejectedObservations.observation: TDD compliance observation - unclear if strict red-green-refactor was followed from transcript skeleton
rejectedObservations.reason: Ambiguous. Worker's handoff claims compliance (followedProcedure: true). Transcript skeletons are summaries, not full evidence. Cannot verify TDD adherence from available artifacts.

rejectedObservations.observation: Admin test files follow a consistent mocking pattern for next-auth
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented implicitly by the test files themselves. The pattern is consistent and discoverable. Adding it to AGENTS.md would be overly prescriptive for an internal testing detail.

rejectedObservations.observation: User model lacks explicit accountStatus field, so admin users page derives status from role
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a schema design decision made in the foundation milestone, not an admin-panel issue. The derived approach is the correct pragmatic solution given the current schema.
